records.ety: [F. dranger; pref. d- = ds- (L. dis) + ranger to range. See Range, and cf. Disarrange, Disrank.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Deranged (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Deranging.]

defs: To put out of place, order, or rank; to disturb the proper arrangement or order of; to throw into disorder, confusion, or embarrassment; to disorder; to disarrange; as, to derange the plans of a commander, or the affairs of a nation.
To disturb in action or function, as a part or organ, or the whole of a machine or organism.
quotes: A sudden fall deranges some of our internal parts.

defs: To disturb in the orderly or normal action of the intellect; to render insane.
